test_cases.title: Organization admin successfully creates a new project within the organization
preconditions: A valid organization-level admin account exists and is authenticated
The organization exists in the system
steps: Authenticate as the organization-level admin
Send a request to create a new project within the organization (e.g., POST /organizations/{orgId}/projects with valid project configuration payload)
Capture the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a success response (e.g., HTTP 201) and the new project is created and retrievable within the organization.

test_cases.title: Organization admin successfully deletes an existing project within the organization
preconditions: A valid organization-level admin account exists and is authenticated
At least one project exists within the organization
steps: Authenticate as the organization-level admin
Send a request to delete an existing project (e.g., DELETE /organizations/{orgId}/projects/{projectId})
Capture the response status
Attempt to retrieve the deleted project
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a success response (e.g., HTTP 200 or 204) and the deleted project is no longer retrievable.

test_cases.title: Organization admin successfully manages a user's organization membership
preconditions: A valid organization-level admin account exists and is authenticated
At least one non-admin user account exists within the organization
steps: Authenticate as the organization-level admin
Send a request to modify a target user's organization membership (e.g., PUT /organizations/{orgId}/members/{userId} with updated membership payload)
Capture the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a success response and the target user's organization membership reflects the updated state.

test_cases.title: Project admin successfully manages their own administered project
preconditions: A valid project-level admin account exists and is authenticated
The project-level admin is assigned as admin for a specific project (projectA)
steps: Authenticate as the project-level admin
Send a request to read or modify projectA's configuration (e.g., GET /projects/{projectAId} and PUT /projects/{projectAId} with valid payload)
Capture the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a success response and the project-level admin is able to view and update projectA's configuration.

test_cases.title: Project admin is denied access to a project they do not administer
preconditions: A valid project-level admin account exists and is authenticated
The project-level admin is assigned as admin for projectA only
A separate project (projectB) exists within the same organization
steps: Authenticate as the project-level admin of projectA
Send a request to read projectB's configuration (e.g., GET /projects/{projectBId})
Capture the response status and body
Send a request to modify projectB's configuration (e.g., PUT /projects/{projectBId} with a payload)
Capture the response status and body
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden) for both the read and modify requests on projectB, and no data from projectB is exposed.

test_cases.title: Project admin is denied the ability to create or delete projects within the organization
preconditions: A valid project-level admin account exists and is authenticated
The project-level admin is assigned as admin for a specific project only
steps: Authenticate as the project-level admin
Send a request to create a new project within the organization (e.g., POST /organizations/{orgId}/projects with a valid payload)
Capture the response status
Send a request to delete an existing project other than their own (e.g., DELETE /organizations/{orgId}/projects/{otherProjectId})
Capture the response status
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden) for both the create and delete requests, and no project is created or deleted.

assumptions: The system exposes RESTful API endpoints for project and membership management; specific endpoint paths may differ from those used as examples.
Authentication is handled via a token or session mechanism, and role assignments (org-level admin, project-level admin) are already configured in the system prior to testing.
A project-level admin has no implicit organization-level admin privileges, and the two roles are mutually exclusive unless explicitly granted.
Managing a project includes at minimum reading and updating project configuration; the full set of manageable project properties is not defined in the requirement.
Managing organization membership includes operations such as adding, removing, or changing roles of users within the organization.
Project-level admins are also restricted from managing user organization membership, as that capability is described only for organization-level admins.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) what 'configure' a project entails — the specific configurable attributes are unknown, making exhaustive configuration test cases impossible; (2) whether a project-level admin has any read access to other projects or is completely blinded — the test case conservatively tests for HTTP 403 on both read and write; (3) whether an organization-level admin retains all project-level admin capabilities implicitly, or if these are separate permission sets; (4) what happens when a project admin's project is deleted — their access state post-deletion is untested; (5) whether there is a super-admin or system-admin role above organization-level admin that should be excluded from scope.
